metadata.title:
Ia FOLLETTE
AhOCUMMINS


OFFER HELP

GREAT FIGHT ON FLOOR OF
SENATE WHEN RATE BILL


COMES UP.

pages.raw_text:
Aided by such men as Senators
Cummins and LaFollette, the SEN
ators from the western and noun.
, tain states promise to put up a
great fight on the floor of the
United States senate when the
short haul. long haul" phase of
the railroad bill comes up.


The Spokane chamber of COM
merce has sent kilograms to Sen
ators Jones and Hepburn and Con-
gressmen Poindexter and Hamer
urging them to stand for justice
for the Inland Empire in securing
law prohibiting charging more
for a short haul than a long haul.
kilograms have also been sent to
the commercial bodies of Is of the
larger Inland Empire cities urging
them to do the same thing.


The fight on the floor of the sen.
ate is expected to be a spectacular
one. Senators Cummins, LaFol-
lette, Hepburn and Smoot are
slated to speak for the provision
prohibiting the higher charge for
the shorter haul. Senator Piles of
Washington will reply, on behalf
of the coast cities, against the bill.


Senator Dixon of Montana will
reply to Piles. Dixon is quoted as
expressing his confidence that the
provision will be adopted by the
senate.
